channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Trusts are a 'breakfast buffet' for reducing tax. Is that about to change?
item.description: Tuesday's federal budget is set to contain changes to the rules for trust funds, which economists say are a popular vehicle for tax planning.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jim CHalmers is poised to unveil changes to the taxation of trusts in Tuesday's budget.

item.title: Trump wanted an American pope but not this American pope
item.description: Foreign leaders and lobbyists thought they would write the next chapter in Catholic history. They failed and Leo is proving to be the pope that some of them feared.
item.dc:creator: Andrew West

media:group.media:description: Foreign leaders and lobbyists thought they would write the next chapter in Catholic history. They failed and Leo is proving to be the pope that some of them feared.

item.title: Removing capital gains tax discount will be 'disastrous' for small business
item.description: Some entrepreneurs argue any changes to reduce the capital gains tax discount should carve out the start-up sector. However, tax experts warn this would create too much complexity in an already complicated tax system.
item.dc:creator: Nassim Khadem

media:group.media:description: Sally-Ann Williams says a change to the CGT discount would make it harder for startups to attract talent.

item.title: What the tax reform duo could mean for renters
item.description: With changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax coming in this year's federal budget, we spoke to three experts who explained how this will impact rental prices.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: Changes to CGT and negative gearing have renters worried that prices will increase.

item.title: Australia on cusp of housing downturn as demand softens
item.description: The tight supply conditions that have underpinned the national housing market in recent years are starting to ease, according to property data firm Cotality.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

media:group.media:description: Cotality property analysts warn a housing downturn is looking more and more likely.

item.title: ISIS-linked women willing to take the 'hit' of arrests for their kids' freedom
item.description: When a group of so-called "ISIS brides" boarded a flight from Doha to Melbourne, they took a leap of faith, unsure of what fate awaited them at the end of their journey. But they said they were willing to face arrest for the freedom of their children.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Rollason

media:group.media:description: Part of the group boarded a connecting flight from Doha to Melbourne.

item.title: ABC weekly news quiz: A stranded ship, an underwhelming space show and a Met movie moment
item.description: Don't bother looking up at the sky, it won't be worth it. Instead, try your luck testing your news knowledge from the past week.

media:group.media:description: Sabrina Carpenter during the Met Gala, an annual fundraising gala held for the benefit of the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute with this year's theme 'Costume Art' in New York City, New York, US, May 4, 2026.

item.title: Sir David Attenborough turns 100: Celebrate his life in his words
item.description: Natural history broadcaster Sir David Attenborough turns 100 today. We look back at his work and interviews to capture some of his wisdom and endless sense of wonder.
item.dc:creator: Peter de Kruijff

media:group.media:description: Sir David Attenborough in the Natural History Museum in London.

item.title: Urzila Carlson thinks your mum is funny, and you should too
item.description: Urzila Carlson's start in comedy was an accident, but now the stand-up regular is bringing sketch comedy to our TV screens.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Safioleas

media:group.media:description: Urzila Carlson says if you don't find women funny you are a "poor bastard".

item.title: Ukraine launches major drone attack amid rows over Victory Day ceasefire
item.description: The Kremlin is taking extraordinary measures ahead of Russia's marking of the defeat of Nazi Germany, as Ukraine launches its second-biggest attack of the war so far.

media:group.media:description: Security in Moscow has been beefed up ahead of the city's commemoration of Victory Day.

item.title: UK police investigated over handling of Al-Fayed sexual offending allegations
item.description: The UK's Independent Office for Police Conduct says it is working to establish whether there were missed opportunities to stop Mohamed Al-Fayed as far back as 2008.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Morgan and Syan Vallance

media:group.media:description: More than 150 women have come forward with allegations against Mohamed al Fayed.

item.title: UK immigration officer guilty of spying on politicians for China
item.description: Two UK-Chinese nationals have been found guilty of spying for China, using UK police systems to gather information.

media:group.media:description: Chung Biu Yuen and Chi Leung (Peter) Wai have been found guilty of spying on behalf of Hong Kong.

item.title: Man arrested after allegedly threatening Andrew on dog walk
item.description: Police arrest a man who allegedly had a weapon and was threatening King Charles III's brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor near his Norfolk home on Wednesday evening, local time.
item.dc:creator: Riley Stuart

media:group.media:description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor moved to Norfolk in February.

item.title: Rubio visits the Vatican after Trump's attacks on Pope Leo XIV
item.description: The secretary of state was seeking to mend fences after US President Donald Trump's social media broadsides against the pope for his opposition to the Iran war.

media:group.media:description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio jokes with Pope Leo XIV as they exchange gifts at the Vatican on Thursday.

item.title: Wasp that eats host alive named after Attenborough for his 100th birthday
item.description: Taxonomists from London's Natural History Museum who named the Chilean species were inspired by the lifework of the famous broadcaster and naturalist.
item.dc:creator: Peter de Kruijff

media:group.media:description: Attenboroughnculus tau is a type of parasitic wasp from Chile that was identified in the London Natural History Museum collection.

item.title: Hawthorn 'taught a bit of a lesson' after Fremantle's fourth-term blitz
item.description: Fremantle overcomes a 15-point deficit to beat Hawthorn thanks to a dominant final quarter.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Fremantle overran Hawthorn in the final quarter to claim a famous win at Perth Stadium.

item.title: Concerns for competency of woman rescued in outback then arrested
item.description: Concerns have been raised about the competency of an elderly Indigenous woman facing a charge of unlawful wounding from 2024, after she was rescued and then arrested by police in Western Australia's outback this week.
item.dc:creator: Jarrod Lucas and Katrina Tap

media:group.media:description: Tjuntjuntjara is 700 kilometres north-east of Kalgoorlie.

item.title: More hantavirus cases expected as passengers prepare to disembark ship
item.description: A number of countries are scrambling to trace people who disembarked from an Atlantic cruise struck by a deadly virus outbreak before it was marooned off the coast of Cabo Verde, in an effort to prevent further spread of the disease.

media:group.media:description: MV Hondius, carrying a number of passengers suspected of having hantavirus infections, leaves Cabo Verde on Wednesday.

item.title: Two men guilty of Alice Springs teenager's New Year's Day murder
item.description: Two men have been found guilty of murder over the death of 19-year-old Yiel Deng Gatluak outside Alice Springs, while their teenage co-accused has been found guilty of the lesser offence of violent act causing death.
item.dc:creator: Chris Murrkarany Fitzpatrick

media:group.media:description: Two men have been found guilty of murdering 19-year-old Yiel Gatluak outside Alice Springs in 2024.

item.title: Ted Turner's infamous feud with Rupert Murdoch began in a Hobart boathouse
item.description: The two media moguls shared a bitter rivalry for decades after some drunken comments at a Hobart boathouse. Here's how their rivalry unfolded through the years.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Steedman

media:group.media:description: Ted Turner publicly feuded with Rupert Murdoch for decades.

item.title: Senior doctors write to Queensland's premier demanding cultural change
item.description: More than 100 senior clinicians from a major regional hospital raise concerns in a letter to the state government about hospital culture.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Dansie and Baz Ruddick

media:group.media:description: Townsville Hospital and Heath Service's CEO says a consulting firm had been engaged to improve the experience of staff.

item.title: Dogs coach says they're 'an easy target' for referees after fourth straight loss
item.description: The Dolphins recover from a rough start to hand the Bulldogs a fourth straight loss as coach Cameron Ciraldo bemoans decisions made by his players and the referees.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: The Bulldogs have slipped to a 3-6 record after one win from their past seven games.

item.title: 'We come together in unity': Nationwide vigils held for Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Vigils have been held across the country in memory of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, who was allegedly murdered in Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Ross, Jesse Thompson, Meg Whitfield, Monte Bovill, Tessa Mapstone, Elsie Lange, Sarah Maunder, and Pip Waller

media:group.media:description: Crowds gathered at vigils filled with pink and candles for the five-year-old girl.

item.title: Brisbane Lions player banned for four weeks for homophobic slur
item.description: Brisbane Lions player Koby Evans is banned for four games after being found guilty of conduct unbecoming for using a homophobic slur against an opponent in the VFL.

media:group.media:description: Lions player Koby Evans has apologised after receiving a four-match ban for making a homophobic slur in the VFL.

item.title: NSW woman seeks damages after alleging doctors amputated healthy leg
item.description: The woman says her leg was amputated despite it being "salvageable", but doctors argue the amputation was necessary to save her life.
item.dc:creator: Giselle Wakatama

media:group.media:description: Vesna Zvicer is seeking damages from Hunter New England Local Health District.
